title: AI Learns About Genes from Many Living Things
summary: Scientists made a smart computer program called Evo 2 that learns about genes from all types of living things, like bacteria, tiny archaea, and bigger plants and animals. It was trained on trillions of tiny parts of DNA called bases. This helps it find important spots in genes, like where genes start and stop or how they are controlled, which can be hard for people to see. The program is open source, meaning anyone can use it for free. It works by looking at patterns in DNA, similar to how our brains learn from lots of examples. This can help scientists understand genes better and discover new things about how living things work.

keywords.explanation: parts of DNA that tell cells how to make proteins or do jobs
a long molecule that holds instructions for living things
a program that is free for everyone to use and share
repeating shapes or sequences that the AI learns to recognize
people who study and learn about the world through experiments

questions.question: How does Evo 2 learn from DNA?
options: By looking for patterns in sequences

background_read: Genes are like tiny instructions inside all living things, made of DNA. Scientists use computers to study genes and learn how they work. AI, or artificial intelligence, is a type of computer program that can learn from lots of data, similar to how we learn from practice. In this article, scientists made an AI called Evo 2 that looks at genes from many different living things to find important parts, helping us understand life better.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists and a team behind the AI program Evo 2
WHAT: An open source AI trained on trillions of DNA bases to find gene features
WHERE: In computer systems used for scientific research
WHY: To help understand genes better and make discoveries about living things

perspectives.description: Evo 2 shows how AI can advance science by analyzing complex genetic data.
